danger_signals.visual_form: Supervillain sprites (Red Skull's assembled army, henchmen)
Distinctive villain sprites (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones, Red Skull)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; major combat challenge ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Final boss sprite with distinctive appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Maximum threat; world domination plot climax

safety_signals.visual_form: Support character sprites (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor the Sub-Mariner)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary assistance available; reduced immediate danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Four selectable heroes with distinct visual designs (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision)
safety_signals.meaning: Player control and agency; protective capability

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character-specific visual identity (Captain America shield motif, Iron Man armor, Hawkeye archer gear, Vision energy form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Side-scrolling combat arenas designed for hero traversal and multi-directional combat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red Skull branding; generic henchmen visual design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Specialized villain designs reflect unique powers (Sentinel's mechanical form, Living Laser's energy appearance, Juggernaut's unstoppable mass visual)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level layouts present as infiltration/conquest objectives (world takeover plot)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active invasion/world takeover plot by Red Skull
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level progression through enemy-occupied territories; increasing villain encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating difficulty; introduction of stronger villains and specialized henchmen as players advance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four Avengers available for selection; supporting heroes appear mid-mission
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiplayer cooperative gameplay (up to four players simultaneously in arcade version); support characters assist in combat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sequential boss encounters signal narrative progression toward final confrontation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss patterns and difficulty escalate; final Red Skull battle represents climactic world-state resolution

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through Red Skull's assembled supervillain army (Klaw → Living Laser → Whirlwind → Sentinel → Wizard → Grim Reaper → Mandarin → Juggernaut → Ultron → Controller → Crossbones → Red Skull) visually communicates the scope and organization of the invasion threat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character-specific gameplay variants communicate individual hero capabilities
environmental_storytelling.example: Captain America and Hawkeye use flying machines for aerial sections; Iron Man and Vision fly independently, establishing power hierarchy and faction differentiation through mechanical design

environmental_storytelling.technique: Side-scrolling brawler layout as infiltration narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Level design progression implies movement through Red Skull's territory/strongholds toward final confrontation, with terrain reflecting military/fortress occupation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hand-to-hand combat, projectile attacks (shield/arrows/energy beams), special 'Avenger Attack' ability, aerial combat variants (flying kick or 45-degree energy beam)
Melee and ranged opposition; generic enemy archetype requiring defeat
Individual combat patterns; unique visual/mechanical presentations reflecting source material powers (Sentinel's mechanical design, Ultron's energy signature, etc.)
Appear temporarily during missions; provide combat assistance reducing immediate threat level

notes: NES version differs significantly from arcade beat 'em up: platformer paradigm rather than brawler. Visual communication relies on character sprite differentiation, enemy type variety, and boss escalation to communicate threat level and narrative progression. The game's world-state (Red Skull's invasion) is communicated through sequential enemy encounters rather than environmental transformation. Marvel license provides established character/faction visual language (Avengers colors, villain iconography). Limited source data available for NES-specific visual analysis; observations extrapolated from gameplay mechanics and character roster.
sources_used: Wikipedia: Captain America and the Avengers (gameplay section, character descriptions, NES version details)
